brief_justification: Paper A is an empirical, information-theoretic characterization of ranking time series (permanence distributions, chart-similarity decay, rank-turbulence divergence, entropy, trajectory clustering) with no generative model, whereas Paper B is a mechanistic stochastic evolutionary-game analysis of the Hawk-Dove game using timescale separation and demographic noise to explain monomorphism vs. polymorphism; the only overlap is the generic theme of temporal/'stochastic' dynamics and persistence, not a shared governing process, so no role-for-role mapping holds.
